_comment: Source: Michigan MDHHS Section 2-6 Spinal Injury Assessment (Revised 05/22/2023, MDHHS Reviewed 2023). Base Michigan template — MCA-specific selections not configured. Source: NASEMSO Clinical Guidelines.
protocol_reference: Michigan Trauma and Environmental Protocol Section 2-6: Spinal Injury Assessment — MDHHS Approved 5/22/23, Reviewed 2023. Source: NASEMSO Clinical Guidelines.

sections.title: Spinal Injury Assessment
points: Assess the mechanism of injury: negative mechanism does NOT require a spine injury clinical assessment; patients with mechanism of injury WITH the potential for causing spine injury SHALL have a spine injury clinical assessment performed
Clinical criteria are used as the basis for assessment — if ANY clinical criteria are present OR if the assessment cannot be completed, the patient has a POSITIVE spine injury assessment

If mechanism with potential for spine injury exists, assess the following clinical criteria: (A) altered mental status; (B) use of intoxicants; (C) a painful injury that distracts the patient from assessment of the spine; (D) motor and/or sensory deficit; (E) spine pain and/or tenderness
If ANY clinical criteria are present → POSITIVE spine injury assessment → maintain spinal precautions during movement and transport per Spinal Precautions-Procedure Protocol
If NONE of the clinical criteria are present → NEGATIVE spine injury assessment
Patients over the age of 65 with evidence of a head strike mechanism of injury WILL have a rigid extrication collar applied even if the spinal injury clinical assessment is negative

deterioration_flags: New or worsening motor/sensory deficit — positive spine assessment; maintain precautions
Altered mental status in trauma patient — automatically positive spine assessment; cannot be cleared
Intoxicated patient — cannot clear spine; treat as positive assessment
Patient age ≥ 65 with head strike — apply rigid collar regardless of assessment result
scope_notes: Spine injury clinical assessment: all levels
Rigid extrication collar application: all levels
Spinal precautions during movement and transport: all levels — refer to Spinal Precautions-Procedure Protocol
